title: Regimen feedback event
description: One event in the append-only JSONL buffer the Feedback capture hook appends to. See docs/event-schema.md for the event contract.

schema_version.description: Versioned semantic-convention marker. Identifies the event shape so readers can evolve the schema without ambiguity.

timestamp.description: When the event occurred, RFC 3339 in UTC. Event time for the log; the span boundary time when paired into a span.

session_id.description: Harness-native session identifier. Groups events into one trace. Stable across compactions for the life of one harness session.

harness.description: The agent harness that fired the event, as a normalized lowercase identifier. A harness listed here is a valid value of the convention; it does not assert that a capture adapter for it exists yet.

model.description: The model that ran the turn this event belongs to, as a normalized identifier, e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7' or 'gpt-5.2'. Recorded per event, never per session: a harness with a model router (Cursor 'Auto', Gemini 'Auto') resolves a different model from one turn to the next. When a router is in use this is the resolved underlying model, not the router label. Optional: a harness may not expose the model on every event, and no model is resolved before the first turn of a session.

cwd.description: The working directory the harness session ran in, as the harness reported it. A session-level anchor for which body of work a conversation belongs to: the loader projects it onto the conversations rollup so a reader can tell conversations apart by where the work happened. Carried on the event only as transport; not stored per-event row. Optional per 'Honest over tidy': a harness that does not expose a directory leaves it absent rather than fabricating one.

trace_id.description: OTLP-native trace id: 16 bytes as 32 lowercase hex chars, derived from session_id. Every event of a session shares it, so events of one conversation group under one trace. It is the only id the capture hook assigns.

span_phase.description: Whether this event opens a span (start), closes a span (end), or is instantaneous (point). Determined by event_type; carried explicitly as the harness-agnostic span-boundary marker.

span_name.description: Human-readable span label, e.g. 'session', 'user_prompt', 'tool:Edit', 'compaction'. A tool's tool.pre and tool.post carry the same span_name.

attributes.description: Event-specific key/value detail. tool.pre and tool.post carry tool_name and tool_call_id; a skill invocation also carries skill_name; gate.denial carries gate_id, tool_name, tool_call_id, and an optional reason; session.end carries end_reason_normalized (always) and end_reason_native (when the harness exposes one).

tool_name.description: The tool invoked, present on tool.pre and tool.post events and on gate.denial, e.g. 'Edit', 'Bash', 'Skill'.

skill_name.description: The slug of the skill a skill invocation ran, e.g. 'tdd'. Present on a tool.pre (and tool.post on the hook path) when the tool call is a skill invocation, read from the harness's skill-tool input field. Absent on every other tool call. Harness-agnostic: any harness whose skill tool names the skill is captured under this one attribute.

tool_call_id.description: Harness-native tool-call id. Identical on a tool's tool.pre and tool.post, so downstream readers pair them into one span; on a gate.denial it is the id of the denied call, correlating the denial with its tool.pre.

gate_id.description: Stable identifier of the discipline gate that denied a tool call, present on gate.denial events, e.g. 'rm-rf-guard'. Free-form: any gate names itself, with no enum of known gates.

reason.description: Why a discipline gate denied a tool call. Optional detail on gate.denial events.

end_reason_native.description: The harness-native session-end reason, preserved verbatim as the harness reported it, present on a session.end event when the harness exposes one (e.g. Claude's 'prompt_input_exit'). Absent when the harness exposes no end reason (e.g. Codex, whose session.end comes from the rollout tailer). Free-form: it is the harness's own value, not normalized.

end_reason_normalized.description: The normalized, harness-agnostic session-end reason on a session.end event, drawn from the minimal vocabulary the implemented harnesses produce (see docs/event-schema.md). 'user_exit' is a deliberate operator exit (clean); 'cleared' is a reset in place; 'other' is the explicit catch-all for an unrecognized or absent native reason. The vocabulary extends additively as new harness adapters land. A session.end always carries this; an event from before this field existed simply lacks it and remains valid.
